rootCauseAnalysis.summary: Intentional memory leak via a 'leaker' sidecar container writing 1MiB/5s to a RAM-backed emptyDir volume (medium:Memory, sizeLimit 256Mi), causing progressive memory exhaustion predicted to hit the pod's 192Mi leaker container limit. Both replicas (on separate nodes) are accumulating leaked memory at ~12MiB/min with no restarts yet, making OOM kill or node memory pressure inevitable without intervention.
contributingFactors: The 'leaker' container runs an infinite loop using 'dd' to write 1MiB random data every 5 seconds to /data (mounted as a RAM-backed emptyDir volume with medium:Memory)
The emptyDir volume is backed by host RAM (medium:Memory) with a sizeLimit of 256Mi — every byte written consumes node memory, not disk
The leaker container has a memory limit of 192Mi vs a request of 32Mi (Burstable QoS), making it a candidate for OOM kill once memory climbs
The emptyDir sizeLimit of 256Mi exceeds the leaker container's own memory limit (192Mi), so the volume could grow until the container itself is OOM-killed
Two replicas are running on separate nodes (kubernaut-demo-worker and kubernaut-demo-worker2), multiplying memory pressure across nodes
Pod QoS class is Burstable, making it an eviction candidate under node memory pressure before Guaranteed pods
No remediation history exists for this deployment — this is the first incident for this workload

kaResponse.analysis: Intentional memory leak via a 'leaker' sidecar container writing 1MiB/5s to a RAM-backed emptyDir volume (medium:Memory, sizeLimit 256Mi), causing progressive memory exhaustion predicted to hit the pod's 192Mi leaker container limit. Both replicas (on separate nodes) are accumulating leaked memory at ~12MiB/min with no restarts yet, making OOM kill or node memory pressure inevitable without intervention.
